This AGREEMENT between [CLIENT NAME] (herein after “CLIENT”) and Megalodon Marketing,
LLC (herein after “INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR”) is entered into and shall commence on November
17th,
2017.
1. TERM OF AGREEMENT
This agreement will be effective on November 17th, 2017 and continue until completion of agreement term.
This agreement may also be terminated in accordance with the provisions of sections 8 and 9 of
this agreement.
2. INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR STATUS
It is the express intention of the parties involved that the “CONTRACTOR” is an INDEPENDENT
contractor and not an employee. This contract does not create an employee relationship between the client
and the contractor. Both the client and the contractor agree that for tax purposes the contractor is not an
employee of the client.
3. SERVICES TO BE RENDERED BY THE CONTRACTOR
The contractor has agreed to perform the following specific services as stated in the Description of Services
attached.
4. METHODS FOR PERFORMANCE
The Contractor, being an independent contractor, is free to choose the methods, details, and means
of performing the above specified services. The Contractor may, at his own expense, employ any assistance
deemed by the Contractor to be necessary to perform the services. Client has no control over or right to
supervise any assistance Contractor deems advisable to employ. The Contractor shall perform the services
required by this agreement at any location and at such times as the Contractor shall determine
without interfering with any schedule already set up by Client or Contractor.
5. COMPENSATION FOR SERVICES RENDERED
Compensation for this project will be collected in the form of a $3,000 ongoing monthly rate for
services rendered by [YOUR COMPANY NAME]. The agreement will commence upon completion of the
$3,000 service fee payment paid in full via payment instruction below. There will also be a 10% revenue
share of net profits generated that will be sent from [CLIENT NAME] to [YOUR NAME] at the end of
every calendar month, only for profits generated from actions taken by [YOUR COMPANY NAME] for the
duration of the active agreement with [YOUR COMPANY NAME] and [CLIENT NAME]. The agreement
will not start until the strategy session with [YOUR NAME] and [CLIENT NAME] occurs after the 1st
payment has been made to confirm SOW below.
Payments can be made in wire and should be made payable to:
Name on account: [YOUR COMPANY NAME] LLC
Bank Name: Bank of America
Routing Number:
Account Number:
Should either party require it, payment schedule is subject to modification and will require an amendment of
this agreement.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 8 --

6. MODIFICATION OF SERVICES
Changes to the original services agreed upon will require an amendment of this AGREEMENT. In the event
that there is a difference in cost, the payment schedule must be modified as well to reflect the new cost. The
most current AGREEMENT will supersede any prior AGREEMENT. Verbal agreements WILL NOT
supersede any written Agreement. All changes MUST be written and amended in the AGREEMENT.
7. CLIENT OBLIGATIONS
The client must establish all relevant details and requests necessary for the performance of the Contractor
prior to commencement of work. 	If Client makes additional requests that affect the process in which the
work is being completed, additional fees may apply and the AGREEMENT must be modified. The Client
must provide CLEAR instructions and specifications and MUST make sure that these are written
and UNDERSTOOD by the Contractor.
8. AUTOMATIC TERMINATION OF THIS AGREEMENT
This agreement will terminate automatically on the occurrence of any of the following events:
a. Bankruptcy of the Contractor or Client.
b. Sale of the business by either party.
c. Death of either party.
9. TERMINATION AT DISCRETION OF PARTIES
The parties may terminate the agreement for any of the following reasons:
a. Termination by the Client for default of Contractor. The Client may terminate the agreement at
any time after the Contractor fails to respond to a written notice of default and demand to perform.
b. Termination by Contractor for breach by the Client. The Contractor may terminate the agreement
at any time after the Client fails to respond to a written notice that he/she is in breach of
the agreement and he/she is requested to cure.
c. Termination for failure to meet agreed upon payment schedule.
d. Termination by CLIENT by either (1) Not paying Fee to initiate a new Term, or (2) Providing
written notice of Termination to INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. 	In either case, CLIENT has
not breached AGREEMENT shall not suffer any penalty.
This is an intangible marketing service that cannot be canceled or revoked. Once invoice is paid, buyer
agrees there are no cancellations, returns, or refunds of any sort because of the nature of services rendered,
provided that all work is completed per this AGREEMENT and the attached Description of Services.
10. GENERAL PROVISIONS:
All work will be completed in a professional manner according to standard practices. Any alteration or de-
viation from specifications above involving extra costs will be executed only upon written order and will
become an extra charge over and above the estimate of this AGREEMENT. All work created will be turned
over to Client and Client retains sole ownership. Contractor retains the right to use work produced as part of
Contractor’s portfolio for future references.
11. SEVERABILITY:
If any provision of this AGREEMENT is held to be illegal, invalid, or unenforceable under present or future
laws effective during the term hereof, such provision shall be fully severable; and this AGREEMENT shall
be construed and enforced as if such illegal, invalid, or unenforceable provision had never comprised a part
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 8 --

hereof; and the remaining provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect and shall not be affected by
the illegal, invalid, or unenforceable provision or by its severance herefrom. Notwithstanding any law to
the contrary, the severance of any illegal, invalid or unenforceable provision shall not affect CLIENT’s and/
or affiliates' right to obtain money damages for INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR’s violation of any re-
maining enforceable provision(s). Furthermore, in lieu of such illegal, invalid, or unenforceable provisions
there shall be added automatically as a part of this Agreement a provision as similar in terms to such illegal,
invalid, or unenforceable provision as may be possible and be legal, valid, and enforceable and that shall
not be more restrictive than the one severed herefrom.
12. NONINTERFERENCE WITH THIRD-PARTY RIGHTS:
INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR warrants and represents that, in entering into this AGREEMENT
and performing the obligations imposed by this AGREEMENT, INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR is not
violat- ing any contractual, fiduciary, or other legal duty which INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR
may owe to some other person with respect to confidential information or relationship. 	The CLIENT and/
or affiliates have no interest in using any other company's or person’s proprietary, confidential, or trade
secret informa- tion in an unlawful manner. INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR agrees that INDEPENDENT
CONTRAC- TOR will not misapply the property of any other company or person to such information by
accepting re- tainment by the CLIENT and/or affiliates or in the course of the relationship with the CLIENT
and/or affili- ates.
13. COMPLIANCE WITH LAWS:
Each Party warrants and represents that it will comply and will cause its affiliates, contractors and subli-
censees to comply with all applicable laws in connection with the performance of its obligations and other
activities pursuant to this AGREEMENT, including without limitation, truth in product promotion and ad-
vertising laws, business opportunity statutes, The Federal Trade Commission Act of 1914, as amended, and
U.S. Patent and Trademark statutes in its marketing, distributing, and selling efforts in connection with this
Agreement.
Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this AGREEMENT, neither Party nor any of its affiliates shall
be required to take, or shall be penalized for not taking, any action that is not in compliance with such Par-
ty's 	ethical 	business 	practices 	and 	policies 	or 	that 	such 	Party 	reasonably 	believes 	is 	not 	in
compliance with applicable laws.
14. NOTICES:
All notices required under this Agreement shall be made in writing and shall be deemed given (1) during
INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR’s retainment, when delivered via interoffice mail or electronic mail and
(2) following termination of business relationship, when posted by certified mail, return receipt requested,
with proper postage prepaid and properly addressed, to the applicable Party's last known address.
15. RELATED PARTIES:
This AGREEMENT shall inure to the benefit of, and be binding upon, the CLIENT and its parents or sub-
sidiaries, joint ventures, clients, and affiliates, together with their successors and assigns, and INDEPEN-
DENT CONTRACTOR, together with INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR’s executor, administrator, per-
sonal representative, heirs, and legatees.
16. SEVERABILITY:
This AGREEMENT represents the entire Agreement of the Parties. This AGREEMENT merges and super-
sedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, undertakings, covenants, or conditions, whether or oral or
written, express or implied, between the CLIENT and INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR.

-- 4 of 8 --

DESCRIPTION 	OF 	SERVICES 	TO 	BE 	RENDERED AFTER 	PAYMENT 	FOR 	TERM ARE AS
FOLLOWS:
Purpose: The intention of the services being rendered ongoing by [YOUR COMPANY NAME]
for [CLIENT NAME] and [YOUR COMPANY NAME] entering this relationship are to drive leads,
sales and upsells through a sales funnel as well as provide [CLIENT NAME] with content
creation and distribution on his social media channels. This is a multi step action plan driving
cold leads into a follow up ad- vertising model to convert them into course buyers after 8-30
brand exposures through curated videos meant to drive engagement an intent while educating
and entertaining the targeted audi- ence(s). Our intentions are to create the funnel assets within
click funnels and cold/retargeting advertising across Facebook and Instagram as well as
creating and posting content for organic social media distribution.
Scope of Work: After payment is made in full for the service fee cost and the agreement is exe-
cuted a strategy session will take place with [CLIENT NAME] and [YOUR COMPANY NAME] to
coordi- nate specific actions, assets needed, or any information/calibration needed to be
exchanged for success in the relationship. After this initial strategy session takes place a majority
of client rela- tionships [YOUR COMPANY NAME] has entered require the client to send high
quality photos, histori- cal links to speed up our copywriting and creative processes, videos that
may need created or found (videos will be sent or shot and gathered in mid Nov according to
[CLIENT NAME]) and shared with the [YOUR COMPANY NAME] staff and upon these assets
being gathered, [YOUR COMPANY NAME] starts executing on the plan on action for asset
creation in addition to what we gather with the fulfilling the same intentions.
The plan of action is to create an information product that is going to include lead page, sales
page, up-sell page, and a general receipt confirmation page.
We recommend a $1,000-$5,000 advertising budget on top of our service fee’s for the first 30 day
cycle for immediate content distribution and then increasing towards the end of the 30 day cycle
as we’ll start advertising the sales offer for the course created in the first half of the 30 day cycle
with the intention of driving sales from all those who engaged with the content from the content
distribution efforts.
While marketing efforts and advertising dollars are going towards cold advertising campaigns, re-
targeting campaigns driving leads, sales and upsells we will also be posting once a day on Face-
book and Instagram for a content distribution strategy and execution plan that furthers our efforts
of keeping, building and maintaining the perspective we have worked hard to generate in those
exposed to ads.
Location of Work: Remote. My team and I are located in Miami and Beverly Hills. Some of my
team members outsource their work to our preferred subcontractors that handle smaller cycles for
us but we alert before hand in labor intensive cases for certain tedious tasks. This will not be one
of those deals, we will handle all work in house and will always notify for login notifications and
general security awareness upon doing any outsourcing and will advise.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

Period of Performance: Funnel creation, course creation, and ad creation will take 15-20 busi-
ness days and will go live after [CLIENT NAME]’s approval. After the creation process finalizes,
the service budget that went towards to initial creative process shifts to those servicing the
account daily posting content, reviewing and managing advertising campaigns, making real time
changes as leads and sales are exposed to assets that can changed or improved in real to
increase per- formance. The relationship is over a 30 day ongoing period of time to create
assets, market to drive leads and the intention to drive sales, along with producing and
distributing ongoing content on social media channels.
Deliverables Schedule: Funnel creation, and ad creation will take 15-20 business days and will
go live after [CLIENT NAME]’s approval. After the creation process finalizes, the service budget
that went towards to initial creative process shifts to those servicing the account daily posting
content, reviewing and managing advertising campaigns, making real time changes as leads and
sales are exposed to assets that can changed or improved in real to increase performance. The
relationship is over a 30 day ongoing period of time to create assets, market to drive leads and the
intention to drive sales, along with producing and distributing ongoing content on social media
channels.
However, here's the order we'll knock everything out:
• 	Start gathering and creating content and get approved behind the scenes. Megalodon Market-
ing team are creating image quotes, video clips, and all content along with posting on social.
• 	registration page for web training or a mini course / preview of the product we’re shooting
comes after [YOUR NAME] and [CLIENT NAME]s initial strategy session to go over details
of what can go on landing page and can be used for education marketing.
• 	Facebook ad images and writing the ad copy along with any videos needing to be created and
provided by [CLIENT NAME] and what Megalodon team can source.
• 	getting everything approved or making changes and final approval after launching the ads, dri-
ving people qualified potential leads to the registration page and then following up with those
who register and those who don't register with contextual retargeting to get them back to doing
what we need them to do in our sales cycle
• 	tweaking everything in real time we've created to further optimize the effectiveness of our con-
versions
• 	Go over the marketing flow and call to actions, software training, etc with [CLIENT NAME],
make any changes in real time to the lead magnets, content, and offers or marketing
assets needed
• 	follow up via ads, email and verbal with any [CLIENT NAME] staff designated for franchise
sales from leads generated and some potentially sold in real time
Applicable Standards: We have to stay away from saying anyone including you can or has
made certain amounts of income or revenue in particular time tables. Meaning in our ads we can
say "from $150M in revenue" but couldn't say "$150M in revenue over 1 year" for example. Just
FYI though.
Acceptance Criteria: If we generate leads from who we'll be targeting to come into the web train-
ing, you'll benefit either way if you have a sales staff that can follow up and create relationships to
eventually close after web training is hosted. If we create quality assets that generate you leads,
and eventually sales we've done our jobs well. If we generate leads that turn to sales without hav-
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

ing a sales team needed, we've out performed and achieved the desired outcome and will contin-
ue optimizing actions to further those results.

each week un- til a 10% conversion is hit, time to consult with them before hand to train and
tweak videos, and someone to approve the assets created along with initial strategy session time
needed.
Also need to be approved and added as page admin on FB page we will be running ads from.
Can send instructions from Facebook upon request. Email to add as admin to both is [YOUR
EMAIL]
